skill.name: deploy-to-vercel
skill.description: Deploy applications and websites to Vercel. Use when the user requests deployment actions like "deploy my app", "deploy and give me the link", "push this live", or "create a preview deployment".
skill.summary: Deploy projects to Vercel with automatic framework detection, git integration, and preview URL generation.
skill.author: vercel-labs

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 168 static findings across 3 files (901 lines). All external command patterns in SKILL.md are documentation examples, not vulnerabilities. Environment file access (lines 204-205) is security-positive—the script explicitly excludes .env files. Network access is limited to Vercel's official deployment API. The skill demonstrates proper security practices and is safe for publication.

high_findings.title: Environment File Access in Deploy Scripts
high_findings.description: The deploy scripts read .env file access at lines 204-205. Upon inspection, these are SECURITY-POSITIVE patterns that explicitly EXCLUDE .env files from deployment packages to prevent credential leaks. The tar commands use --exclude='.env' and --exclude='.env.*' to protect sensitive credentials from being deployed.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct inspection of lines 204-205 confirms the script explicitly excludes .env files from deployment packages, which is a security best practice.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Substitution in SKILL.md
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md file contains bash code blocks with backtick syntax for displaying command examples. These are documentation examples, not actual code execution. The static scanner incorrectly flags markdown-rendered bash examples as Ruby/shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is a markdown documentation file. All command patterns are bash code blocks within markdown fences, not executable code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Flag
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 'description' metadata and 'git commit -m' as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are false positives - 'description' is a YAML metadata field, and 'git commit -m' is a standard version control command, not cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML frontmatter 'description:', and line 68 is 'git commit -m'. Neither involves cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Commands
low_findings.description: Commands like 'vercel whoami' and 'command -v vercel' are standard pre-flight checks to determine if the Vercel CLI is installed and authenticated. These are legitimate checks required for the skill to function.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These commands check CLI availability and authentication status, which is required behavior for a deployment skill.

content.user_title: Deploy your project to Vercel instantly
content.value_statement: Manually deploying projects to Vercel takes time and requires navigating the dashboard. This skill automates the entire deployment workflow with automatic framework detection and preview URL generation.
actual_capabilities: Deploy projects to Vercel via CLI with automatic framework detection for Next.js, Astro, Remix, SvelteKit, and 40+ other frameworks
Support multiple deployment methods including git push, Vercel CLI direct deploy, and no-auth fallback for sandboxed environments
Generate preview URLs and claim URLs for transferring deployments to user accounts
Check Vercel CLI authentication status and guide users through login if needed
Link projects to Vercel teams with proper scope configuration for multi-team environments
Handle deployment failures with troubleshooting guidance for network issues and auth problems
limitations: Cannot deploy to production unless the user explicitly requests it (skill defaults to preview deployments)
Requires network access to Vercel API endpoints for deployment operations
Cannot bypass Vercel authentication or deploy to teams the user does not have access to
The no-auth fallback method requires users to claim their deployment through a URL

use_cases.title: Quick Preview Deployment
use_cases.description: A developer wants to share their latest changes with a client before merging to main. They ask Claude to deploy their Next.js project and get a shareable preview link in seconds.

use_cases.title: Automated Git Push Deployments
use_cases.description: A team lead wants to ensure their entire team can deploy from any branch. They ask Claude to link their project to Vercel and enable automatic preview deployments on every push.
use_cases.target_user: Development teams using Vercel git integration

use_cases.title: Multi-Framework Support
use_cases.description: A developer working on an Astro site needs to deploy quickly. The skill automatically detects Astro from their package.json and deploys with the correct configuration.
use_cases.target_user: Developers using various static site generators and frameworks

prompt_templates.title: Basic Deployment
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to quickly deploy their current directory to Vercel

prompt_templates.title: Production Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy my project to production on Vercel
prompt_templates.scenario: User explicitly requests a production deployment

prompt_templates.title: Deploy with Team Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy this project to my company team on Vercel
prompt_templates.scenario: User belongs to multiple Vercel teams and needs to specify which one

prompt_templates.title: Git Push Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Link this project to Vercel and set up automatic deployments when I push to git
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to configure git-based continuous deployment

output_examples.output:
Deployment successful!

Preview URL: https://my-project-abc123.vercel.app
Claim URL: https://vercel.com/claim-deployment?code=xyz

View your site at the Preview URL.
To transfer this deployment to your Vercel account, visit the Claim URL.

This project is connected to Vercel via git. I can commit and push to trigger a deployment. Want me to proceed?

[After user approval]

Linked and deployed!
Preview URL: https://my-project-abc456.vercel.app

best_practices: Always deploy to preview first to verify changes work in production-like environment before merging to main
Use git push method when possible for automatic deployments and proper rollback capabilities
Let Claude handle team selection when you have multiple Vercel accounts to avoid deploying to wrong team
anti_patterns: Do not deploy to production unless explicitly requested - preview deployments prevent accidental disruptions
Do not skip authentication checks - ensure the Vercel CLI is properly authenticated before attempting deployment
Do not deploy without checking git status - always commit changes before git push deployment method

faq.question: What happens to my .env files when deploying?
faq.answer: Environment files are automatically excluded from deployment packages to prevent credential leaks. Your .env files stay local.

faq.question: Can I deploy without logging into Vercel?
faq.answer: Yes, the no-auth fallback method deploys your project and returns a preview URL plus a claim URL. Visit the claim URL to transfer the deployment to your account.

faq.question: Which frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: The skill auto-detects 40+ frameworks including Next.js, Astro, Remix, SvelteKit, Nuxt, Gatsby, React, Vue, and many more from your package.json.

faq.question: How do I deploy to a specific team?
faq.answer: When you belong to multiple teams, Claude will present team options. Select one and all subsequent commands use that team scope.

faq.question: Why did my deployment fail with a network error?
faq.answer: On claude.ai, add *.vercel.com to your allowed domains in settings. On Codex, you may need to rerun with escalated network permissions.

faq.question: How do I get a production deployment URL?
faq.answer: Use 'deploy to production' or 'deploy to prod' - only these explicit phrases trigger production deployments. Preview is the default.
